paragraphs: From nothing, an illusion.[#pause] That's what power is.[#pause] The ruler is but a slave to the system. Its most visible servant.[#pause] A name and a face to mock.
That's the big joke, and Ulre would never get it.[#pause2] After all, the Savior is sacred.[#pause] Beyond mockery.

sections.heading:
18 CLICKS POST UNFOLDING 
OFFICER QUARTERS PANTRY FNS BRISINGER
paragraphs: Alistair Hale sat on a crate with a small bottle of frazzer and a pack of crunchy peanuts. Across from him, Lena Hague frowned, running another diagnostic on her medical pulsor.
Marcus Carlson was pacing up and down the pantry. He had shaved the stubble from his jaw, revealing a gaunt face, but one less haunted. He had regained a hint of his old authority as XO again.

paragraphs.text: "[steady]It's quieted down out there," he reported.
paragraphs.speaker: marcus-carlson

paragraphs: A figure appeared around the corner. A young crewman, probably not even twenty, with a headful of chestnut curls running down past his shoulders. Alistair didn't recognize him at first. But then realized it was Jason Galindo, a holo-media technician who occasionally hosted creative performances. He moved in a strange, dreamlike way, skipping as if he heard music no one else could.
His uniform jacket was inside out and open. He stopped near their door, noticed them, and gave a conspiratorial wink. Then he hopped away, humming a strange tune.

paragraphs.text: "[muttered]Jason the fucking jester. How jolly," Alistair muttered into his bottle. "[muttered]Still as mad as a mingled box of bolts."
paragraphs.speaker: alistair-hale

paragraphs.text: "[skeptical]Are they, though?" Marcus stopped his pacing and looked at Alistair and Lena. "[skeptical]They're not unreasonable. Corbin's coup. His Grid.[#pause] That's a much colder kind of madness."
paragraphs.speaker: marcus-carlson

paragraphs.text: "[grim]All demons," Alistair said, "[grim]from different tribes of hell."
paragraphs.speaker: alistair-hale

paragraphs.text: Marcus shrugged. "[resigned]Maybe."
paragraphs.speaker: marcus-carlson

paragraphs.text: Lena Hague looked up from her pulsor. "[analytical]The *Bris* is still on course for the orbital anchor. Another six, seven clicks, what happens then?[#pause] What happens when Gupta and our 'crew' arrive in Mairee's orbit?"
paragraphs.speaker: lena-hague

paragraphs.text: "[grim]Quarantine," Marcus said. "[grim]He could try to stop us from docking altogether."
paragraphs.speaker: marcus-carlson

paragraphs.text: "[low]They're already inside us," Adrian muttered. "[probing]All of us. Even you, Hale.[#pause] You went through it. What was it like exactly, your transit through the Fold?"
paragraphs.speaker: adrian-farley

paragraphs: Alistair flicked a peanut across the pantry. It skittered into a corner.

paragraphs.text: "[evasive]I dunno. Goofy."
paragraphs.speaker: alistair-hale

paragraphs: A faint blush rose on his neck. It was clear there was more to it, but he changed the subject.

paragraphs.text: "[redirecting]Westhoff aims to keep the Directorate in the dark."
paragraphs.speaker: alistair-hale

paragraphs.text: "[concerned]That's short-term," Marcus said. "[concerned]Once we reach orbit, they'll see for themselves that something's off. We're a plague ship, headed straight for—"
paragraphs.speaker: marcus-carlson

paragraphs: As he spoke, the humming of the Jason became audible, and he skipped back into view. He stopped in front of them and held up a finger as if saying, 'Watch this.' Then he bent his leg backward at the knee in one smooth, fluid motion, [#soft-click]producing a soft click, followed by a faint, rubbery creak.

paragraphs.text: "[startled]What the hell?" Alistair jumped to his feet.
paragraphs.speaker: alistair-hale

paragraphs: Jason made a show of lowering his pants. His penis was tucked between the petals of an orchid-like flower growing directly from his skin. It was bright orange and purple. It was throbbing. Jason let out a low, odd moan, and the flower puffed a small cloud of yellow pollen. Alistair heard a [#squelch]wet squelching sound, as if someone were squeezing an overripe peach. Jason dropped to the floor and inhaled the cloud with a loud [#sniff]sniff. His body went stiff in a brief, ecstatic spasm. Then the color drained from his face, and his mouth tightened as if he were swallowing something back.
Alistair grabbed his pistol, and [#pistol-whine]the weapon's charge indicator whined to life. Jason squeaked in surprise, pulled up his pants, and hopped away.

paragraphs.text: Alistair lowered his pistol, disbelieving. "[disbelief]How very fucking reasonable," he said. "[sarcastic]All quiet out there, my ass…"
paragraphs.speaker: alistair-hale

paragraphs: [#female-scream]A scream echoed down the hall. It was Stacey's. Alistair ran out of the pantry, weapon ready again. He turned the corner and saw her pressed against a bulkhead. She looked disgusted. Jason pulled up his pants again while hopping around the far end of the corridor, flopping his leg around. Stacey's hand shook as she pointed, her eyes wide with horror.

paragraphs.text: "[horror]He… he just…"
paragraphs.speaker: stacey-kiran

paragraphs.text: "[smirking]Yeah." Alistair smirked. "[jesting]We saw. Get me the flamethrower," he jested.
paragraphs.speaker: alistair-hale

paragraphs: He leaned toward Stacey.

paragraphs.text: "[gentle]Hey. You alright?"
paragraphs.speaker: alistair-hale

paragraphs.text: "[snapping]I'm fine," she snapped, still shaky. "[shaky]Just processing an anomalous event."
paragraphs.speaker: stacey-kiran

paragraphs.text: "[redirecting]You know what we need?" Alistair nodded toward the training room down the hall. "[playful]Smashout rematch!"
paragraphs.speaker: alistair-hale

paragraphs: It was a transparent attempt to redirect her attention. Stacey's disgust was instantly replaced by a competitive fire.

paragraphs.text: "[challenging]I'll beat your ass this time, Heyoka," she challenged.
paragraphs.speaker: stacey-kiran

paragraphs: They cleared the rummaged Smashout court. The walls bore marks from countless matches. Smashout was a fast-paced racket game, something between tennis and padel, played against a three-dimensional grid of glowing panels.

The aim was to smash out a full row on your opponent's back wall. A lucky hit could even restore one of your own squares—if it matched the same color. Once a row was gone, the match entered Smashout. From that point on, a single clean shot to the back grid ended the game.
Alistair killed the main lights. Colorful gridlines lit the court as they coded their panels. [#shirt-buttons-pop]He unsealed his flight suit, fingers working the clasps.

paragraphs.text: "[casual]Alright, hold up," he said, tugging his arms free and letting the suit fall. "[casual]I won't sweat my suit any more than I have. Try not to get distracted."
paragraphs.speaker: alistair-hale

paragraphs: Underneath, he wore tight dark gray boxer briefs. Stacey rolled her eyes and smirked. She [#zipper]unzipped her uniform and stepped out of it smoothly. After folding her jumpsuit and placing it on a crate, she stood in only her black compression bra and matching shorts.

paragraphs.text: "[teasing]You're the one who should be worried.[#pause] Less padding for a body shot."
paragraphs.speaker: stacey-kiran

paragraphs: The gridlines shone on their skin. Stacey picked up the hard rubber ball and tossed it to Alistair, who snatched it from the air.

paragraphs.text: "[firm]Standard rules," she said. "[firm]And this time, Heyoka, no more 'creative' interpretations of the rules.[#pause] Play clean."
paragraphs.speaker: stacey-kiran

paragraphs: Alistair grinned, took position, and squared his shoulders.

paragraphs.text: "[grinning]You wound me, Abacus. My play is always creative.[#pause] You're already staging excuses."
paragraphs.speaker: alistair-hale

paragraphs: He served. [#thwack]The yellow neon ball shot off his racquet at a sharp angle. Stacey was ready, pushing off the floor and twisting in midair to meet it. [#thwack2]She sent it back with a loud thwack.

For the first few pulses, they didn't speak, lost in the physical chess match. He was all power, but she countered it with athletics. Alistair's body was a collection of lean, efficient lines, built for high-G endurance. His shoulders were broad but not bulky, and his muscles tightened with every shot.
Stacey was smaller and leaner. Her legs were strong and defined, letting her push off surfaces with [#footsqueek]quick twists. Her short dark hair spun, tracing a halo.
Sweat gathered on Alistair's forehead as he worked off his tension. The madness.
He scored. A red panel on Stacey's side went dark.

paragraphs.text: "[panting]Your coverage is too low, Abacus," he panted.
paragraphs.speaker: alistair-hale

paragraphs.text: "[focused]Check your own footwork," she shot [#panelhit]back and hit a blue panel.
paragraphs.speaker: stacey-kiran

paragraphs.text: "[dismissive]That was luck," he said.
paragraphs.speaker: alistair-hale

paragraphs.text: "[smug]That was geometry." She grinned.
paragraphs.speaker: stacey-kiran

paragraphs.text: "[breathy]So," he said, catching his breath. "[curious]That flower thing…"
paragraphs.speaker: alistair-hale

paragraphs: Stacey slowed and shuddered.

paragraphs.text: "[shuddering]It's like it was part of him."
paragraphs.speaker: stacey-kiran

paragraphs.text: Alistair smiled. "[wry]I've seen worse."
paragraphs.speaker: alistair-hale

paragraphs: He served.

paragraphs.text: "[skeptical]Liar," she said, reacting fast.
paragraphs.speaker: stacey-kiran

paragraphs: His focus wavered at her accusation. He lunged for the return but [#woosh-thud]collided with the wall and missed. The purple panel in the top left blinked out.
He recovered, pushing off the wall.

paragraphs.text: "[grinning]I once joined a riot control unit against a mob of nude exo-environmentalists."
paragraphs.speaker: alistair-hale

paragraphs: He stopped in the center of the court.

paragraphs.text: "[quiet]Yeah," he said quietly. "[admitting]And I got tanked. Seen some crazy shit in there."
paragraphs.speaker: alistair-hale

paragraphs: He moved back to his spot. Frustrated, he grabbed a new ball and [#thwack]racked the serve. The ball twanged, barely clearing the net, but Stacey caught it anyway. [#]Alistair leapt, stumbling, but returned it. Stacey was ready and smacked it into his far-left corner. [#panelhit]Another of his panels went dark. A green one.

paragraphs.text: "[unsteady]The Tank," she said, her voice a bit unsteady. "[admitting]I spent some time there, too."
paragraphs.speaker: stacey-kiran

paragraphs: Alistair froze, staring at her across the net.

paragraphs.text: "[surprised]Wait," he muttered. "[surprised]You…"
paragraphs.speaker: alistair-hale

paragraphs.text: "[soft]Yes," Stacey said, soft but clear. "[soft]Addiction rehabilitation.[#pause] Three months."
paragraphs.speaker: stacey-kiran

paragraphs: Alistair bounced the ball, caught it, and served again, [#netserve]listlessly hitting the net. The game paused. They approached the net. A charged silence hung between them as they locked eyes. Stacey snorted, her laugh breaking the tension.

paragraphs.text: "[playful]You're up. Let's go, 'slomo'!"
paragraphs.speaker: stacey-kiran

paragraphs: The game resumed with a new intensity. For another pulse, there was no sound but grunts and the thwack of the ball. Alistair started to heat up, scored twice, but then he [#footsqueek]slipped on a patch of his own sweat where he had fallen before. Stacey didn't hesitate and [#thwack]slammed the ball into his exposed side. She knocked another one out [#panelhit]while retrieving one of her own. He walked to the net.

paragraphs.text: "[wincing]Dirty, Stace."
paragraphs.speaker: alistair-hale

paragraphs.text: "[grinning]You taught me," she shot back, grinning.
paragraphs.speaker: stacey-kiran

paragraphs: They played several more rallies as Alistair found his rhythm and caught up. After an intense rally, he slipped again. Stacey aimed for the kill shot but hesitated and held back. She obviously regretted it right away. He recovered and smashed the ball hard. It [#rimclip]hit the rim of her racquet, spun oddly, and clipped the last corner of a panel. The last one in the row.

[#smashout]All of Stacy's panels on her side flashed with swirling neon colors. The grid lines disappeared, replaced by a wall of light. She was now in 'Smashout,' and that meant her entire wall became the final target.

paragraphs.text: "[shouting]Smashout, Abacus!" Alistair shouted.
paragraphs.speaker: alistair-hale

paragraphs: It was her serve. He didn't swing. Instead, he exploded off the floor toward the ball and intercepted it with [#ball-smack]his chest. He gasped. He kicked the ball midair across the net. It wasn't a powerful kick, but it was completely unexpected, and the rules allowed it. The ball arced lazily. Stacey moved to intercept it, but she slipped off-balance. The ball bounced and gently tapped her wall.
[#claxon]BWWAAAAH. The buzzer rang. Her wall of light flashed a brilliant, solid red, then went completely dark. Game over.
They met at the net in seconds, both flushed and breathing hard. Stacey clearly wasn't happy.

paragraphs.text: "[whispered]You went easy on me and made me believe I had a chance," she whispered.
paragraphs.speaker: stacey-kiran

paragraphs.text: "[soft]Never."
paragraphs.speaker: alistair-hale

paragraphs: He reached out and brushed a damp strand of her dark hair from her face as the net stowed in the floor between them.

paragraphs.text: "[hesitant]Why did you spend time in the Tank?" Stacey asked a little hesitantly.
paragraphs.speaker: stacey-kiran

paragraphs.text: "[evasive]Maybe for something silly. Maybe something terrible," he said, his eyes dropping to her lips.
paragraphs.speaker: alistair-hale

paragraphs: She stepped closer, wrapping her hand around his neck and pulling him into a deep kiss. With his hand pressed firmly against her waist, he pulled their bodies tight together. She moaned softly, both demanding and giving in, her fingers sliding down his chest.
He broke the kiss and rested his forehead against hers.

paragraphs.text: "[whispered]Stace," he whispered.
paragraphs.speaker: alistair-hale

paragraphs: She didn't answer. Instead, she pressed her lips to his sweaty neck. She pulled back slightly as if to say something, to either stop it or push it further. Stacey ran her hand through his damp hair. Their lips met again. Alistair's hand moved down her neck, shoulder, and back, lower each time.

paragraphs.text: "[murmured]Why the Tank, really?" she murmured, ending the kiss.
paragraphs.speaker: stacey-kiran

paragraphs: He hesitated.

paragraphs.text: "[heavy]Faulty sensor. Bad judgment.[#pause] I was piloting a speeder at a wedding ceremony. Lost control. It clipped a support arch, which sent us spinning through the crowd.[#pause] Someone got hurt badly. The bride. Still can't walk.[#pause2] It should've been a celebration. Best day.[#pause] Turned out to be the worst.[#pause] Doc said the Tank might help me forgive myself."
paragraphs.speaker: alistair-hale

paragraphs: She pressed closer, her cheek against his.

paragraphs.text: "[tender]You still carry it."
paragraphs.speaker: stacey-kiran

paragraphs.text: "[admitting]Yeah," he admitted. "[soft]But not now. Not with you."
paragraphs.speaker: alistair-hale

paragraphs: Stacey closed her eyes.

paragraphs.text: "[confessing]Well. My turn.[#pause] The addiction. Sleep-cycle boosters, stress suppressors, anything to keep the edge. Even Zups.[#pause] I would've lost my slot if it wasn't for Westhoff."
paragraphs.speaker: stacey-kiran

paragraphs: He kissed her temple, gently.

paragraphs.text: "[tender]You came back sharper. Braver."
paragraphs.speaker: alistair-hale

paragraphs: She smiled. They leaned into each other. And whatever waited beyond the court had lost its gravity.

Marcus Carlson stood at the pantry counter. He held a flask and tilted it so a single drop fell onto the polished steel. In the ship's gravity, the drop held its shape for a moment, a shimmering bubble, before it collapsed. He let another drop fall, and another.

Across from him, Lena Hague cared for a small hydroponics tray. A few weak sprigs pushed up through the nutrient gel under the soft lamp. She used her medical scissors to carefully snip a browning leaf.
In the corner, Adrian Farley lay on a thin cot. His hands rested behind his head. His eyes were closed. He wasn't asleep, just lost in thought.

paragraphs.text: "[lilting]The little Colors are stretching their legs," Marcus said, his voice ringing higher than usual. "[observant]That pilot, Hale. He's erratic, explosive. Blunt."
paragraphs.speaker: marcus-carlson

paragraphs.text: Lena misted the tray. "[amused]But easy to read.[#pause] And easier still to look at."
paragraphs.speaker: lena-hague

paragraphs.text: "[soft]The girl…" Adrian murmured, his eyes still closed. "[contemplative]She, too, has notes in the margins.[#pause] A subtext we've yet to unravel."
paragraphs.speaker: adrian-farley

paragraphs: None of them looked at each other. None of them needed to.

paragraphs.text: "[knowing]They barely feel it." Marcus released another drop. "[knowing]But they will have to attune eventually."
paragraphs.speaker: marcus-carlson

paragraphs.text: Lena gently stroked a tiny leaf. "[curious]And us? Why are we different?"
paragraphs.speaker: lena-hague

paragraphs.text: "[explaining]The Library needed a translator, the Garden a cultivator, and the Gate a keeper," Adrian explained, finally looking up.
paragraphs.speaker: adrian-farley

paragraphs.text: Marcus sighed. "[impatient]Their attunement is too slow."
paragraphs.speaker: marcus-carlson

paragraphs.text: "[patient]Patience, Carlson," Hague said. "[patient]No garden grows overnight.[#pause] We need to nurture them."
paragraphs.speaker: lena-hague

paragraphs: Adrian sat up slowly, his eyes now open. Marcus swept the counter dry with his arm.

paragraphs.text: "[grave]And while we are being patient, our wellspring is running dry."
paragraphs.speaker: marcus-carlson

paragraphs: As he spoke, he stared at Lena's hydroponics tray. The small sprigs were no longer struggling. During their conversation, they had grown. Their leaves were now a bright, unnatural green, and a single flower had bloomed blue.
Jason poked his head in. When he saw it was only the three of them, he stepped inside. He moved with grace and pointed a trembling finger at the flower in Lena's tiny garden and whispered,

paragraphs.text: "[reverent]She is waking up."
paragraphs.speaker: jason-galindo

paragraphs.text: "[gentle]Who is waking up?" Adrian asked.
paragraphs.speaker: adrian-farley

paragraphs: Jason's eyes darted toward the ceiling, as if looking through the hull, out into the void.

paragraphs.text: "[reverent]Mommy."
paragraphs.speaker: jason-galindo

paragraphs: He giggled and bent low toward Adrian.

paragraphs.text: "[conspiratorial]The other Colors are blending."
paragraphs.speaker: jason-galindo
